title: Structural Iron and Steel Workers
description: Raise, place, and unite iron or steel girders, columns, and other structural members to form completed structures or structural frameworks. May erect metal storage tanks and assemble prefabricated metal buildings.

tasks.text: Read specifications or blueprints to determine the locations, quantities, or sizes of materials required.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Connect columns, beams, and girders with bolts, following blueprints and instructions from supervisors.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Bolt aligned structural steel members in position for permanent riveting, bolting, or welding into place.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fasten structural steel members to hoist cables, using chains, cables, or rope.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Hoist steel beams, girders, or columns into place, using cranes or signaling hoisting equipment operators to lift and position structural steel members.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Verify vertical and horizontal alignment of structural steel members, using plumb bobs, laser equipment, transits, or levels.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cut, bend, or weld steel pieces, using metal shears, torches, or welding equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Erect metal or precast concrete components for structures, such as buildings, bridges, dams, towers, storage tanks, fences, or highway guard rails.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Force structural steel members into final positions, using turnbuckles, crowbars, jacks, or hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Pull, push, or pry structural steel members into approximate positions for bolting into place.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Unload and position prefabricated steel units for hoisting, as needed.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Drive drift pins through rivet holes to align rivet holes in structural steel members with corresponding holes in previously placed members.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assemble hoisting equipment or rigging, such as cables, pulleys, or hooks, to move heavy equipment or materials.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fabricate metal parts, such as steel frames, columns, beams, or girders, according to blueprints or instructions from supervisors.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Ride on girders or other structural steel members to position them, or use rope to guide them into position.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Hold rivets while riveters use air hammers to form heads on rivets.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Insert sealing strips, wiring, insulating material, ladders, flanges, gauges, or valves, depending on types of structures being assembled.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Place blocks under reinforcing bars used to reinforce floors.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
